sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.exact_document_title: Self-harm: assessment, management and preventing recurrence
sources.jurisdiction: England and Wales; requires UAE legal and pathway adaptation
sources.population: Children, young people and adults who have self-harmed, unless a recommendation is age-specific.
sources.clinical_setting: Community, emergency department, hospital and mental-health care following self-harm.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for compassionate psychosocial assessment, emergency-department initial assessment, immediate safety, safeguarding, willingness for physical and mental care, care-plan status, observation and discharge documentation after self-harm. It does not apply a risk score, predict suicide or generate treatment.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.5.1–1.5.17 — psychosocial assessment and care
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports recording the person’s needs, concerns, strengths, vulnerabilities, context and clinician-performed psychosocial assessment without using a prediction score.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.7.12–1.7.20 — emergency-department initial assessment and care
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting injury severity, emotional and mental state, distress, immediate safety, safeguarding, willingness for care, observation and care-plan status in the ED.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.3.1–1.3.2 and 1.9.6 — safeguarding and discharge
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports recording safeguarding consideration and clinician-confirmed assessment, plan, communication and follow-up before discharge after self-harm.

sources.exact_document_title: Mental Health in Emergency Departments: A toolkit for improving care
sources.version: Mental Health in EDs Toolkit, last revised April 2023
sources.jurisdiction: United Kingdom emergency medicine; UAE law and pathways govern local use
sources.population: Adults and children presenting to emergency departments with mental illness, self-harm risk or mental-health crisis.
sources.clinical_setting: Emergency mental-health triage, initial assessment, documentation, observation and liaison working.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for documenting clinician-performed mental-health triage, current risk, agitation, capacity to leave, prior psychiatric history, mental-state examination, safeguarding and liaison referral. It does not predict future suicide or authorise detention, restraint or treatment in the UAE.

exact_sections.heading: Mental-health audit standards for the emergency department
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting triage, current self-harm or suicide risk, leaving risk, capacity for a decision to leave, prior psychiatric history, mental-state examination and referral or follow-up when performed.

exact_sections.heading: Mental Health Triage and Initial Assessment
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting current presenting state, agitation, immediate safety, safeguarding, willingness to accept care, observation level and care-plan status; it explicitly warns triage is not a future-risk prediction tool.
